name: John B's Oysters Bienville
recipeIngredient: 6 Dozen Oysters (Opened, Reserving Liquid And Shells)
Tabasco, salt and white pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. In a saucepan, sauté green onions, butter, and garlic over low heat until the butter melts.
2. Gradually add flour to the pan while stirring continuously to prevent lumps from forming.
3. Cook the mixture on low heat until it becomes bubbly and forms a smooth roux.
4. Slowly combine the oyster liquid, mushroom liquid, and enough heavy cream to make a total of 1 1/2 cups of liquid in a bowl.
5. Gradually whisk the creamy liquid into the roux, stirring constantly over low heat until the sauce is smooth and thickens.
6. Stir in lemon juice, white wine, chopped parsley, Tabasco, salt, and white pepper to taste.
7. Simmer the mixture for 15 minutes on low heat, stirring occasionally.
8. Place a bed of rock salt (1 to 2 inch layer) in a large Pyrex baking dish.
9. Arrange oyster shells onto the bed of salt.
10. Carefully place one oyster into each half shell.
11. Bake at 375 degrees Fahrenheit for about 15 minutes, or until the edges of the oysters curl.
12. Use a meat basting bulb to drain any liquid from the individual oysters.
13. Spoon the sauce over each oyster and sprinkle with grated fresh Parmesan cheese.
14. Return the dish to the oven and broil for an additional minute or two until lightly browned, being careful not to burn.
15. Serve hot with lemon wedges and toast points.
